violations.text: Chorizo and tortillas were observed being held past the date of disposition. Chorizo and tortillas were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout the establishment and on shelves. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to continue to work with a licensed pest control company to eliminate pests. Establishment is to increase pest control as frequently as needed. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings with appropriate means of sanitizing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/20/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/27/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (limes, avocados, sauces) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025

Empanadas were observed held at 104-114 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025

The door to the outside, located at the entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the retail shelving have an accumulation of rodent droppings. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2025

The fans in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dirt, dust and soil residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025
